perception.who_is_this_for_a: A Disney trip planner who wants a quick, safe way to start a free dining reservation alert without giving Disney login credentials.
perception.who_is_this_for_b: A broader mix of Disney dining planners, Annual Passholders, and travel agents who may want proof, comparisons, coverage, and professional paths.

reasoning: Candidate A gets closer to the core job in the first viewport: the headline directly addresses refreshing, the alert setup form is immediately visible, and the no-login plus official Disney handoff trust claims appear beside the CTA. On mobile, A preserves the actual form fields in the first screen area, so a user can understand and begin the primary task quickly. Candidate B has stronger segmentation depth and more expert/comparison content in the full page, including planner types, restaurant coverage, comparison tables, and agency inquiry, but its first viewport is less conversion-direct. On mobile B hides the setup fields behind a generic CTA and shows a large sample alert before the user can see restaurant/date/party-size entry, weakening the primary free-alert path. B also says “Get alerted the second your table opens,” which is emotionally strong but edges closer to overpromising than A’s more careful framing. Overall A better satisfies the high-trust, self-serve, free-alert-first mandate.
blocking_visual_issue_b: Mobile first viewport does not show the actual alert setup fields, and the first action is less explicit than Candidate A’s visible form.
